turns.user_message: Honestly, I don't know why I'm even here. I mean, I know I should quit, but it's hard. I've been smoking for 10 years, and it's just a part of my life. Especially at work, you know? I'm a bartender, and everyone smokes. It's just what we do.
step2_rag_chunks: Avoid situations where others are smoking during the early stages of quitting. Being around smokers can increase the urge to smoke.
Many people smoke out of habit during specific routines such as after meals, while drinking tea or coffee, or when talking with friends.
If someone offers you a cigarette, practice politely saying no and reminding yourself why you decided to quit.

step4_session_phase.phase_instruction: You are in the MOTIVATION phase. You understand their triggers. Now explore their motivation: why do they want to quit — is it health, family, cost, doctor advice? Have they tried before and what happened? Be empathetic. Do NOT push strategies yet.

[Recent Conversation]
Therapist: Hello, how are you? Welcome to EDosthi. I'm here to help you with tobacco cessation. Would you like to tell me about your tobacco use?

Patient: Honestly, I don't know why I'm even here. I mean, I know I should quit, but it's hard. I've been smoking for 10 years, and it's just a part of my life. Especially at work, you know? I'm a bartender, and everyone smokes. It's just what we do.

Therapist: I understand. It sounds like smoking is closely tied to your social life and work environment. Can you tell me more about what makes it hard for you to quit? What are some of the things that come to mind when you think about giving up cigarettes?

Patient: Well, like I said, my friends and coworkers all smoke. And when we're out having drinks, it's just what we do. I don't know how I'd fit in without smoking. And my family, too... they all smoke. I don't want to be the odd one out. Plus, I don't know anyone who's quit, so I don't really have anyone to look up to or get advice from.

Therapist: It sounds like you're feeling a bit isolated in your desire to quit, and that social pressure is a big concern for you. I want to acknowledge that it's tough to go against the norm, especially when it feels like everyone around you is smoking. But I also want to ask, what do you think your life would be like if you were able to quit smoking? What would be some benefits for you?

Patient: I guess... I wouldn't have to worry about my health as much. And I wouldn't have to spend so much money on cigarettes. But it's hard to imagine not smoking when I'm out with friends. I don't know who I'd be without cigarettes. It's weird, but it's like, who am I if I'm not the guy who smokes?
